authors: Al-Kaisy A, Van Buyten JP, Carganillo R, Caraway D, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Panwar C, Rotte A, Amirdelfan K, Kapural L
title: 10 kHz SCS therapy for chronic pain, effects on opioid usage: Post hoc analysis of data from two prospective studies
study_question: Does 10 kHz spinal cord stimulation reduce opioid dosage in chronic pain patients taking high-risk doses?
population_assessed: Chronic low back and leg pain patients on opioids, primarily with failed back surgery syndrome, mean age 51.8 years.
outcome_measures: Opioid dose reduction (MME/day), back pain VAS, leg pain VAS
inclusion_criteria: Chronic back and/or leg pain, VAS ≥5 cm, candidates for spinal cord stimulation, age ≥18 years
answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS reduced mean opioid dose 41% and high-risk >90 MME patients from 39.3% to 23.0% while achieving significant pain relief.
